metadata.title: Claude, Taught
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream; code stays Onda dark glass
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: inner beats pair the spark with one short serif line; typing is B00-only. OUTRO LAW: outro restates the title. Spine: B00 ASK · B01 THE SKILL (Onda code-block on blank ClaudeWindow: real SKILL.md frontmatter) · B02 INFLUENCE HALF (register, taste, pickWhen) · B03 SPECIFY HALF (laws, gates, file contracts) · B04 AMORTIZATION (authored once, runs forever) · B05 FAILURE MODE (vague skill = improvising runner) · B06 ROUTING TIE-IN (Fable authors, Opus runs) · B07 VERDICT (artifact: anatomy of a skill) · B08 title-restate outro.

signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: You just found the file that runs this whole channel. One playbook that knows my register, my gates, my file layouts. Write it once, hand it to any model, and the taste is already in the room. What's actually in it?

props.command: /skill-creator teach claude my way of making videos

beats.narration_text: A skill is one file, two halves. The top tells Claude when to load it. The bottom tells Claude what to do once it does. Almost every vague skill I've seen is just the top half — wearing the word skill like a costume.

props.title: SKILL.md — the anatomy
props.code:
---
name: claude-explainer
pickWhen: user asks for a claude reel
register: Teardown
---

## Laws
- GATE P before any audio spend
- Cold open: always ClaudeComposerAsk
- Spark line on every inner beat
- Outro restates the episode title

beats.narration_text: The top half is the influence section. Register — how it sounds. PickWhen — what triggers it. Taste decisions the author locks in once so the runner never has to guess. The runner reads them and sounds like you, without asking.

The bottom half is the specify section. File contracts — where every output lands. Gates — what must be verified before money is spent. Laws — not suggestions, rules. This half the runner can execute start to finish, no interpretation required.

Fable authored this skill once. Every reel since has paid down that session — Opus following the laws, filling the gates, landing the files exactly where the contracts say. One authoring session. The quality freezes in at authoring time and every run inherits it.

The failure mode looks like a skill but is only vibes. Match my tone. Be thorough. Keep it tight. The runner guesses, the output drifts, and you call it a hallucination. It's not. It's an empty playbook wearing the word skill.

Which is why the authoring model matters. Fable writes the skill — one session where every clause earns its place. Opus runs it, session after session, following the laws. The expensive part was always the authoring. The cheap part is the part that runs forever.

The anatomy, one page. The top half: pickWhen and register — the influence. The bottom half: laws and gates — the spec. Write it in Fable. Run it in Opus. The skill is judgment, written down once, so it never has to be improvised again.

props.artifactTitle: Anatomy of a skill — one page
artifactLines: pickWhen + register — the influence half. When to load it and how to sound.
Laws — the rules the runner never breaks, no matter what.
Gates — what must pass before any money is spent.
File contracts — where outputs land and what they're named.
Author in Fable once. Run in Opus forever. The cheap part is the part that runs.

beats.narration_text: If this explains what a SKILL.md actually does, subscribe. Nik Bear Brown.
